answer: You know, someone whose advice I really value is my older sister. She’s always been like my go-to person whenever I’m unsure about something. I mean, she just has this way of making things clear without, you know, overwhelming me. One piece of advice she gave me that really stuck was to focus on progress instead of perfection. For example, when I was stressing about college applications, she reminded me to take things one step at a time and not compare myself to others. That really helped me stay calm and confident. I think I trust her so much because she’s been through similar experiences, and she’s honest but also super supportive. Honestly, her guidance just makes me feel grounded, and I know she always has my best interests at heart.
title:
Talk about someone whose advice you value.
Who gives you valuable advice?
What advice has helped you most?
Why do you trust their guidance?
extData:

   - "Well, I’d say someone whose advice I really value is [Person]."  
   - "You know, they’re the kind of person who always [Positive Trait about Them]."  
   - "In my opinion, their advice has helped me a lot in different areas of my life."  

2. Body 1 (First Reason/Example):  
   - "One major reason I trust their guidance is that [Reason 1]."  
   - "For example, there was a time when [Specific Example 1], and their advice really helped me to [Result/Outcome]."  
   - "That experience showed me how much I could rely on what they say."  

3. Body 2 (Second Reason/Example):

   - "For instance, once they told me to [Specific Advice], and it turned out to be [Outcome/Impact]."  
   - "Honestly, I think their way of looking at things makes a lot of sense."  

4. Conclusion:  
   - "So, overall, I’d say their advice has been really important to me because [Concluding Thought or Restated Stance]."  
   - "I trust them completely, and I feel lucky to have someone like [Person] to guide me."  "}
